text.title: Speech-to-Text — Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure: scope use case → bench providers on representative clip → pick by SLO + cost → wire fallback → ship + monitor WER.

step.name: scope-use-case
step.action: Declare mode (batch vs streaming), max latency, target WER per language, diarization need, custom vocab list.
step.decision-gate: If both real-time AND privacy-critical AND no GPU available — re-scope; constraints unsolvable (r1-default-provider).

step.name: bench-providers
step.input: 30-min representative clip per language + 3 candidate providers
step.action: Run each provider; compute WER, latency, cost-per-minute, diarization quality. Score each on the constraint vector.
step.decision-gate: If best WER > target by >20% — fix audio quality first; provider switch alone won't close gap (r3-input-validation).

step.name: pick-provider
step.input: bench report + monthly volume + privacy constraint
step.action: Apply decision tree. Pin provider + model + mode. Set chunk + overlap from r4.
step.decision-gate: If volume >500h/month and not on GPU — flag for self-host migration (r2-self-host-break-even).

step.name: wire-fallback
step.input: secondary provider OR local faster-whisper container
step.action: Implement: trigger fallback on consecutive 5xx OR timeout > threshold. Mark Transcript.degraded=true when fallback engaged. Test with synthetic outage.
step.decision-gate: If fallback also fails in synthetic test — escalate; cannot ship without verified fallback (r5-mandatory-fallback).

step.name: ship-and-monitor
step.action: Roll out at 5% → 25% → 100%. Log per-request: provider, latency, WER on monitor-set, fallback flag. Alert on WER drift.
step.decision-gate: If WER drifts >10% over baseline — investigate audio quality OR provider regression.
